name: Parallel Branch Processing
description: Demonstrates how to use parallel branches in Power Automate to execute multiple independent operations concurrently, significantly reducing overall flow execution time.
key_features: Apply to each with concurrency enabled

parallel_branches_concept.description: By default, Power Automate runs actions sequentially. Using parallel branches, you can run independent operations at the same time, reducing total execution time.
parallel_branches_concept.how_to_add: Click the '+' between actions and select 'Add a parallel branch'
use_cases: Sending notifications to multiple channels simultaneously
Fetching data from multiple independent sources
Triggering multiple child flows at once
limitations: Parallel branches cannot share variable writes (use Compose instead)
All branches must complete before the flow continues

flow_steps.action: PARALLEL BRANCHES - Execute all notification channels simultaneously

branches.description: Sends a confirmation email to the customer.
Notifies the operations team in Teams.
Pushes the order status to an external system.

branches.action: HTTP POST to PDF generation service
branches.description: Generates and stores a PDF receipt.

flow_steps.description: Runs after ALL parallel branches complete. Updates the status to 'Processed'.

apply_to_each_concurrency.description: Enable concurrency on Apply to each loops to process multiple items in parallel.
apply_to_each_concurrency.how_to_enable: Click the '...' on Apply to each > Settings > Concurrency Control > On
warnings: Higher concurrency may trigger API throttling (429 errors).
Do not write to the same variable inside a concurrent loop - use arrays carefully.
Test with moderate concurrency first and increase if no throttling occurs.

performance_tips: Use parallel branches for independent operations (notifications, updates, etc.)
Enable Apply to each concurrency for bulk processing (start at 5-10, increase as needed)
Use Filter Query on data connectors to reduce the number of items returned
Use Select action to project only the fields you need before processing
Use Compose actions to pre-compute values instead of recalculating inside loops
Prefer batch operations over individual API calls when the connector supports them
notes: Parallel branches are ideal when operations are truly independent of each other.
If Branch B depends on the result of Branch A, they are NOT parallel - keep them sequential.
Use Compose (not variables) to store intermediate results in parallel contexts.
Monitor flow run times in the run history to identify bottlenecks.
